changes.value:
## What

`dispatch_command_internal` in `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` resolves the board handle from an explicit `board_path` parameter (line 1015). The scope chain already carries `window:label`, and `UIState::window_board(label)` maps labels to board paths. The backend should resolve the board from the scope chain instead of the explicit parameter.

**Change `dispatch_command_internal` (line 1015):**
```rust
// Current:
let active_handle = resolve_handle(state, effective_board_path).await.ok();

// Target: try scope chain first, fall back to explicit param, then active board
let scope_board_path = ctx.window_label_from_scope()
    .and_then(|label| state.ui_state.window_board(label));
let effective = scope_board_path.or(effective_board_path);
let active_handle = resolve_handle(state, effective).await.ok();
```

This is backwards-compatible: if the scope chain has a window moniker, it uses that. If not, falls back to explicit `board_path`. If neither, falls back to active board.

**Files to modify:**
- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` â€” `dispatch_command_internal` board resolution (line ~1015)

**TDD approach:** Write the test first in `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` or a new test module. The test needs an `AppState` with UIState that has a windowâ†’board mapping, a scope chain with `window:test`, and verifies the correct board handle is resolved.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `dispatch_command_internal` resolves board from `window:label` in scope chain via `UIState::window_board`
- [ ] Falls back to explicit `board_path` param when no window in scope
- [ ] Falls back to active board when neither is available
- [ ] All existing commands still work (backwards compat)

## Tests
- [ ] New test: scope chain `[\"window:main\"]` + UIState mapping `mainâ†’/path/to/board` â†’ resolves correct handle
- [ ] New test: scope chain `[]` + explicit `board_path` â†’ resolves from param (fallback)
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` â€” all pass
- [ ] `cd kanban-app && cargo test` â€” all pass
Backend: resolve board from scope chain window moniker

+++ modified
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 ## What

-`dispatch_command_internal` in `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` resolves the board handle from an explicit `board_path` parameter (line 1015). The scope chain already carries `window:label`, and `UIState::window_board(label)` maps labels to board paths. The backend should resolve the board from the scope chain instead of the explicit parameter.
+`dispatch_command_internal` in `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` resolves the board handle from an explicit `board_path` parameter (line 1015). The scope chain already carries `window:label`, and `UIState::window_board(label)` maps labels to board paths. Replace the explicit parameter with scope-chain-only resolution.

 **Change `dispatch_command_internal` (line 1015):**
 ```rust
@@ -7,28 +7,31 @@
 // Current:
 let active_handle = resolve_handle(state, effective_board_path).await.ok();

-// Target: try scope chain first, fall back to explicit param, then active board
-let scope_board_path = ctx.window_label_from_scope()
+// Target: resolve from scope chain window moniker â€” no boardPath param
+let board_path = ctx.window_label_from_scope()
     .and_then(|label| state.ui_state.window_board(label));
-let effective = scope_board_path.or(effective_board_path);
-let active_handle = resolve_handle(state, effective).await.ok();
+let active_handle = resolve_handle(state, board_path).await.ok();
 ```

-This is backwards-compatible: if the scope chain has a window moniker, it uses that. If not, falls back to explicit `board_path`. If neither, falls back to active board.
+When no `window:label` is in the scope chain, `resolve_handle(state, None)` falls back to the active board (single-window/CLI case). The explicit `board_path` parameter is removed from `dispatch_command` and `dispatch_command_internal` signatures.
+
+**Also update the Tauri command signature:**
+- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` â€” `dispatch_command` #[tauri::command]: remove `board_path` parameter
+- Frontend callers already send scope chain with `window:label`; they stop sending `boardPath`

 **Files to modify:**
-- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` â€” `dispatch_command_internal` board resolution (line ~1015)
+- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` â€” remove `board_path` from `dispatch_command` and `dispatch_command_internal`, resolve board from scope chain

-**TDD approach:** Write the test first in `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` or a new test module. The test needs an `AppState` with UIState that has a windowâ†’board mapping, a scope chain with `window:test`, and verifies the correct board handle is resolved.
+**TDD approach:** Write Rust integration tests that set up AppState with UIState windowâ†’board mapping and open board handles. Dispatch commands with scope chains containing `window:test` and verify the correct board is used. Test the no-window fallback (CLI/single-window uses active board).

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `dispatch_command_internal` resolves board from `window:label` in scope chain via `UIState::window_board`
-- [ ] Falls back to explicit `board_path` param when no window in scope
-- [ ] Falls back to active board when neither is available
-- [ ] All existing commands still work (backwards compat)
+- [ ] `dispatch_command` no longer accepts `board_path` parameter
+- [ ] Board resolved from `window:label` in scope chain via `UIState::window_board`
+- [ ] No-window fallback: when scope chain has no window moniker, uses active board
+- [ ] All existing commands still work

 ## Tests
 - [ ] New test: scope chain `[\"window:main\"]` + UIState mapping `mainâ†’/path/to/board` â†’ resolves correct handle
-- [ ] New test: scope chain `[]` + explicit `board_path` â†’ resolves from param (fallback)
+- [ ] New test: scope chain `[]` â†’ falls back to active board (CLI/single-window)
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` â€” all pass
 - [ ] `cd kanban-app && cargo test` â€” all pass
\ No newline at end of file

changes.old_value:
## What

`dispatch_command_internal` in `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` resolves the board handle from an explicit `board_path` parameter (line 1015). The scope chain already carries `window:label`, and `UIState::window_board(label)` maps labels to board paths. Replace the explicit parameter with scope-chain-only resolution.

**Change `dispatch_command_internal` (line 1015):**
```rust
// Current:
let active_handle = resolve_handle(state, effective_board_path).await.ok();

// Target: resolve from scope chain window moniker â€” no boardPath param
let board_path = ctx.window_label_from_scope()
    .and_then(|label| state.ui_state.window_board(label));
let active_handle = resolve_handle(state, board_path).await.ok();
```

When no `window:label` is in the scope chain, `resolve_handle(state, None)` falls back to the active board (single-window/CLI case). The explicit `board_path` parameter is removed from `dispatch_command` and `dispatch_command_internal` signatures.

**Also update the Tauri command signature:**
- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` â€” `dispatch_command` #[tauri::command]: remove `board_path` parameter
- Frontend callers already send scope chain with `window:label`; they stop sending `boardPath`

**Files to modify:**
- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` â€” remove `board_path` from `dispatch_command` and `dispatch_command_internal`, resolve board from scope chain

**TDD approach:** Write Rust integration tests that set up AppState with UIState windowâ†’board mapping and open board handles. Dispatch commands with scope chains containing `window:test` and verify the correct board is used. Test the no-window fallback (CLI/single-window uses active board).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `dispatch_command` no longer accepts `board_path` parameter
- [ ] Board resolved from `window:label` in scope chain via `UIState::window_board`
- [ ] No-window fallback: when scope chain has no window moniker, uses active board
- [ ] All existing commands still work

## Tests
- [ ] New test: scope chain `[\"window:main\"]` + UIState mapping `mainâ†’/path/to/board` â†’ resolves correct handle
- [ ] New test: scope chain `[]` â†’ falls back to active board (CLI/single-window)
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` â€” all pass
- [ ] `cd kanban-app && cargo test` â€” all pass
Backend: resolve board from scope chain window moniker
